summary: No ship: R-2 is resolved, and the RED record matches 7373cdf and fails for the intended helper assertions. However, R-1 remains too shallow at the workflow call site, and R-3 lacks an executable lease protocol. Simpler alternative: none materially simpler than the deterministic branch design; make its workflow state machine and explicit lease testable.

findings.title: Blocker: GREEN can pass without fixing the production workflow
findings.body: The declared regression invokes only `ensure-bump-pr.ts`; neither witness exercises the workflow where query, branch push, and PR creation are ordered. The helper can therefore turn GREEN while `bump-poll.yaml` remains unwired or queries too late. A concrete retry failure is also uncovered: if the deterministic branch push succeeds but `gh pr create` fails, the next poll sees no open PR, chooses `create`, and collides with the orphan remote branch, stalling deployment while both witnesses remain GREEN.
findings.recommendation: Re-pin RED with a workflow call-site contract or hermetic integration test proving the query and decision occur before mutation, `skip` performs no push/create, and a push-success/PR-create-failure retry reconciles an existing branch without an open PR.

findings.title: DIRTY rebuild has no usable force-with-lease precondition
findings.body: The plan requires `--force-with-lease` but neither captures the existing branch head OID nor specifies fetching that branch. The workflow checkout fetches `main`; on a later run, a bare lease push can lack the remote-tracking value needed for the existing DIRTY branch and reject it as stale. Global workflow serialization does not establish a Git lease, so the promised recovery can repeatedly fail and leave the deployment stalled.
findings.recommendation: Include the PR head OID in the observed facts and use an explicit `--force-with-lease=<ref>:<expected-oid>`, or fetch the exact branch before rebuilding. Add characterization for successful rebuild and rejection after an intervening head update.

next_steps: Replace or augment the helper-only RED with workflow-level ordering and retry witnesses.
Specify the deterministic branch state machine for open PR, orphan branch, and DIRTY rebuild states.
Recapture the machine-owned RED verify-record after updating the regression contract.
